mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in falling corporate revenues and rising recession probabilities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE spikes > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with >55% accuracy over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon as profit margins expand.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks and falling factory utilization, causing short-term equity declines as markets anticipate reduced output and inventory drawdowns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with INDPRO declines during recovery phases, or the mechanism predicts negative returns when actual returns are positive in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates real interest rate tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative forward returns as discount rates outpace nominal earnings growth.
failure_condition: The combined inflation and rate signal fails to predict negative returns, with 20-day forward returns turning positive in >58% of triggered instances over a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 5-year period, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sudden collapse in construction demand and related consumer spending, triggering immediate short-term equity sell-offs as markets price in downstream economic weakness.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where HOUST drops >5%, or the threshold signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates labor market inefficiency and supply constraints, which suppresses corporate profit margins and leads to negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts negative returns when actual 20-day returns are positive in >65% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.
